claim_boundary: Exact-row, same-host, bounded throughput snapshot for Llama 3.2 3B Instruct Q8_0 on one Apple M4 (16GB) host. Camelid prefill reads above both llama.cpp and MLX-LM in every round of this session (camelid 576.7/583.3/586.8 vs MLX-LM 575.5/575.4/579.9), with a +1.4% session-median margin that exceeds neither runtime's observed inter-round spread by a wide factor; the table claims a narrow same-session win, not a durable or general one. Not a model-family, context-sweep, or production-load claim.

host.thermal_note: warm host; rounds alternate runtime order to bound order/thermal bias

workload.model_row: Llama 3.2 3B Instruct Q8_0 (GGUF for camelid and llama.cpp; mlx-community Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct-8bit for MLX-LM)
workload.prompt: 601-token repeated-sentence prompt (' '.join(['The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.'] * 60))
workload.camelid_command: camelid bench-generate <model> --prompt-file <601-token prompt> --max-tokens 16 --temperature 0 --warmup --iterations 5 --json (default CLI fast stack)
workload.mlx_command: mlx_lm.generate two-point decode + 601-token verbose prompt pass (mlx-lm, see mlx-version.txt)

method: Three same-session rounds with alternating runtime order (llama.cpp first in round 2, camelid first in rounds 1 and 3). Per-runtime result is the median across rounds; camelid per-round value is the median of 5 measured iterations (1 unmeasured warmup). Per-round detail retained in raw logs in this bundle.

mlx_lm.note: two-point decode from the same mlx bench passes; camelid and llama.cpp decode were not re-measured in this session

parity: Camelid greedy 16-token continuation matches the CPU reference path token-for-token on the bench prompt in every iteration of every round (output_token_ids in the camelid jsonl logs).
